output: 1. Confirm signalment and history (age, weight, hypothyroidism on levothyroxine, recent dog‑park exposure). 2. Perform a focused physical exam: temperature, heart rate, respiratory effort, mucous membranes, abdominal palpation, cardiac and lung auscultation, neurological assessment. 3. Order baseline labs: CBC, serum chemistry (renal, hepatic, electrolytes), free T4/TSH to evaluate thyroid control. 4. Obtain standing thoracic radiographs (right lateral & ventrodorsal) for pulmonary infiltrates, heart size, pleural fluid. 5. If cough persists >48 h or imaging abnormal: perform tracheal wash/BAL cytology and culture; consider PCR panel for canine respiratory viruses. 6. Abdominal ultrasound if CBC/chemistry suggests organomegaly or mass; otherwise abdominal radiographs. 7. Fecal flotation and PCR for Giardia/Coccidia to rule out GI causes of lethargy. 8. Provide supportive care: hydration, appetite stimulants (mirtazapine/cyproheptadine), palatable diet. 9. Initiate empiric broad‑spectrum antibiotics (e.g., amoxicillin‑clavulanate) if bacterial infection suspected pending culture. 10. Communicate with owner about test rationale, expected timelines, possible diagnoses (infectious respiratory disease, thyroid flare, cardiac/hepatic disease), and infection control at dog park.
